_meta.note: Daemon-backed close of one subscription by sub_id, freeing its registry slot (Phase 1, Subscriptions design section 4). Close is IDEMPOTENT and is NEVER an error: closed=true when the handle existed and was removed, closed=false when the sub_id was already unknown (never opened, already closed, malformed, or reset by a daemon restart). A malformed sub_id is treated as simply not present (closed=false), mirroring the registry's close -> bool contract, so unlike subscription_pull/seek a bad handle does NOT raise unknown_subscription here. Freeing a slot is the remedy for subscription_limit_exceeded on a later open.

response_example_already_unknown._note: An unknown, already-closed, or malformed sub_id is closed=false, not an error. Close is idempotent: re-closing a handle the daemon no longer tracks (including after a restart that reset every subscription) simply reports false. There is NO unknown_subscription error on this path -- a bad handle is just absent.

invariants: close is idempotent and never an error: closed=true if the handle existed, closed=false if it was already unknown
a malformed sub_id is treated as not present (closed=false), not unknown_subscription -- unlike subscription_pull/seek
closing a handle reset by a daemon restart reports closed=false
closing frees a registry slot, the remedy for subscription_limit_exceeded on a later open
daemon unavailable is a typed daemon_unavailable error, not closed=false
